etymology.description: Depicts a bundle or bag tied at both ends
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicting a bundle or bag tied at both ends, it suggests convergence or collection, visually resembling a tied shape. This pictorial representation is historically connected to the direction 'east', as a bag captures and holds items, analogous to the rising sun being 'held' in the east. Despite alternative interpretations involving the sun and tree, the pictogram of a bundle remains the most direct visual link.

examples.chinese: 他的東(他的東)里装满了书(裏裝滿了書)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de dōng lǐ zhuāng mǎn le shū.
examples.english: His bag is full of books.

examples.chinese: 她买了一个新的東(她買了一個新的東)。
examples.pinyin: Tā mǎi le yī gè xīn de dōng.

examples.chinese: 在办公室里(在辦公室裏)，他的東(他的東)总是放在椅子后面(總是放在椅子後面)。
examples.pinyin: Zài bàngōngshì lǐ, tā de dōng zǒng shì fàng zài yǐzi hòumiàn.
examples.english: In the office, his bag is always placed behind the chair.
